title: ～か～ないかのうちに
titleEn: Expresses an action that occurs immediately after another action; as soon as, no sooner than, the moment
structure: Verb-casual + か + Verb-negative form + かのうちに
structureEn: Verb-casual + か + Verb-negative form + かのうちに
explanation: Dùng khi hành động B xảy ra gần như ngay sau hành động A, nhanh đến mức A vừa xảy ra hay chưa kịp xong thì B đã xảy ra. Có thể hiểu là “vừa mới... thì...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + か + Verb-negative form + かのうちに.
explanationEn: The ～か～ないかのうちに grammar point is used to express that an action occurs immediately after another action. In English, it can be translated as as soon as, no sooner than, or the moment. This grammar point is used with verbs, showing the verb in both affirmative and negative forms with か and ないか, followed by のうちに
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～かねない
titleEn: Express the likelihood of a negative outcome or consequence
explanation: Dùng để cảnh báo rằng một kết quả xấu có thể xảy ra. Mẫu này không chỉ nói khả năng, mà còn hàm ý người nói lo ngại về hệ quả đó. Cấu trúc: Verb-stem + かねない.
explanationEn: The ～かねない grammar point is used to express the possibility or likelihood of a negative outcome or consequence, often used when there is concern about the result. It is typically used with verbs in their stem form and can be understood as might (have a bad result)
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～かねる
titleEn: Express inability or impossibility of doing an action (often as a polite refusal)
explanation: Dùng để nói không thể làm hoặc khó có thể làm điều gì, thường với sắc thái lịch sự, dè dặt hoặc từ chối mềm. Cấu trúc: Verb-ます stem + かねる.
explanationEn: The ～かねる grammar point is used to express that someone cannot do an action or that something is impossible to do. It typically has a nuance of reluctance or polite refusal and is formed by adding かねる to the ます-stem of the verb
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～かのようだ
titleEn: Express a similarity or comparison; as if, like, as though
structure: Verb-casual + かのようだ, い-Adjective + かのようだ, な-Adjective + であるかのようだ, Noun + であるかのようだ
structureEn: Verb-casual + かのようだ, い-Adjective + かのようだ, な-Adjective + であるかのようだ, Noun + であるかのようだ
explanation: Dùng để so sánh một tình huống với điều khác, như thể nó thật sự là như vậy dù thực tế có thể không phải. Có thể dịch là “như thể”, “cứ như là”. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + かのようだ, い-Adjective + かのようだ, な-Adjective + であるかのようだ, Noun + であるかのようだ.
explanationEn: The ～かのようだ grammar point is used to express similarity or comparison between two things or situations. It can be translated as as if, like, or as though in English. The standard patterns differ depending on whether it’s used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, or nouns. For な-adjectives and nouns, we usually insert である before かのようだ
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～か～まいか
titleEn: Used to express a dilemma or indecision between two options or actions
structure: Verb-volitional + か + Verb-volitional + まいか, い-Adjective + であるか + い-Adjective + であるまいか, な-Adjective + であるか + な-Adjective + であるまいか, Noun + であるか + Noun + であるまいか
structureEn: Verb-volitional + か + Verb-volitional + まいか, い-Adjective + であるか + い-Adjective + であるまいか, な-Adjective + であるか + な-Adjective + であるまいか, Noun + であるか + Noun + であるまいか
explanation: Dùng khi phân vân có làm hay không làm, chọn hay không chọn một việc. Sắc thái trang trọng hoặc văn viết hơn cách nói thông thường. Cấu trúc: Verb-volitional + か + Verb-volitional + まいか, い-Adjective + であるか + い-Adjective + であるまいか, な-Adjective + であるか + な-Adjective + であるまいか, Noun + であるか + Noun + であるまいか.
explanationEn: The ～か～まいか grammar point expresses uncertainty or indecision, often with a more formal or literary feel. Typically, it is seen with verbs in the volitional form plus まい (the negative volitional). In more academic or stiff writing, い／な-adjectives and nouns plus である can also appear before まいか
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～からこそ
titleEn: Express the emphasis on the reason; precisely because, it is precisely because
structure: Verb-casual + からこそ, い-Adjective + からこそ, な-Adjective + だからこそ, Noun + だからこそ
structureEn: Verb-casual + からこそ, い-Adjective + からこそ, な-Adjective + だからこそ, Noun + だからこそ
explanation: Dùng để nhấn mạnh lý do: chính vì A nên B mới xảy ra hoặc mới có ý nghĩa. Sắc thái mạnh hơn から thông thường. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual + からこそ, い-Adjective + からこそ, な-Adjective + だからこそ, Noun + だからこそ.
explanationEn: The ～からこそ grammar point is used to emphasize the reason or cause of a situation, often implying that the effect is especially notable or even surprising. It can be translated as precisely because or it is precisely because
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: 〜からして
titleEn: Expresses that something is exceptional, unusual, or negative based on a particular aspect or point
explanation: Dùng khi chỉ cần nhìn từ một khía cạnh, ví dụ hoặc điểm xuất phát đã có thể đưa ra đánh giá về toàn bộ sự việc. Thường mang sắc thái bất ngờ hoặc tiêu cực. Cấu trúc: Noun + からして.
explanationEn: The 〜からして grammar point is used when you want to make a conclusion or emphasize that something is exceptional (often negative or surprising) from a certain aspect or starting point. The part before からして serves as the base or clue for the judgment
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～からすると
titleEn: Expresses a judgment or evaluation based on a certain standard; from this perspective, considering, judging from
explanation: Dùng để đưa ra nhận định dựa trên một tiêu chuẩn, lập trường hoặc góc nhìn nhất định. Có thể hiểu là “xét từ...”, “nhìn theo...”, “đánh giá từ...”. Cấu trúc: Noun + からすると.
explanationEn: The ～からすると grammar point is used to make a judgment or evaluation based on some criterion, perspective, or standard. It can be translated as from this perspective, considering, or judging from in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
